reviews.notes: [agent:needs_source] Good Pascha hook, but it lacks a usable source URL and can be tighter.
[agent:needs_source] Strong hook, but the support fields are missing and the current claim reaches beyond its source.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Vivid, but it adds an unsourced spiritual claim about enemy rage.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The three-S hook works, but the motive claim about blaming Moses is unsupported.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The straw-as-binding fact claim is not sourced and is not needed for recall.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The Christ-typology line needs source support and makes the aid longer than needed.
[agent:unsupported_claim] It turns into devotional commentary instead of a tight memory hook.
[agent:needs_source] Good Name hook, but it needs a real source URL and a shorter claim.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The seven-theme symbolism is unsourced; the count itself can be memorized more simply.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Good sorting instinct, but it adds an outside claim about Egypt’s gods.
[agent:needs_source] Rich theology, but the public source is missing and the wording is heavy.
[agent:needs_source] Strong covenant line, but it needs direct support and a tighter form.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The breathing image is strong, but “not unbelief” is an interpretive add-on.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The zoom-lens idea is helpful, but the priestly-bloodline claim is unsupported.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The G-K-M core is good, but the Tabernacle-carrying claim goes beyond the row.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The family chain works, but the Ark claim needs sourcing.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The sibling hook is good, but Miriam’s title adds an outside claim.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The name-meaning claim needs a source and is not necessary for memorization.
[agent:keep] Sharp A-M-M mnemonic and the wife-vs-sister contrast is clear.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Luke 1:5 does not support the equivalence claim about Elisheba and Elizabeth.
[agent:keep] Clear root-not-branch contrast and fully tied to the genealogy.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The later strange-fire history is outside the row and unsourced here.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The vowel trick is good, but the strange-fire history needs sourcing.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The hardening analogy and glory claim are teaching claims without a public source here.
[agent:confusing] The A-8-3 hook is good, but the last line muddies the point.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The three-forties chronology is memorable but unsourced; use a cleaner 80 hook.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Cobra-crown and serpent-typology claims need sources and make the aid too loaded.
[agent:unsupported_claim] “Real powers, not sleight of hand” is an unsourced claim beyond the answer.
[agent:confusing] “Fake snakes” may confuse because the text says their rods became serpents; the plague-duel preview is unsourced.
[agent:needs_source] Good teaching hook, but the Hapi/idol-judgment claim needs a public supporting source.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The later-sea foreshadowing and drink-water framing add unsupported context.
[agent:keep] Punchy, accurate, and clearly separates blood-water results from the darkness plague.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The river-god humiliation claim needs a source; the seven-day hook can stand alone.
[agent:needs_rewrite] The “cannot make one drop clean” lesson is a good thought but an unsourced teaching claim.
[agent:needs_source] Strong teaching aid, but Heqet and idol-judgment claims need public source support.
[agent:needs_rewrite] “Prophet/priest” language adds a teaching layer not needed for this recall question.
[agent:confusing] Internal inconsistency: the answer says temples were not listed, but the aid implies frogs entered the idols’ own house.
[agent:keep] Vivid bed-and-bread image that directly reinforces the answer.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The salvation/taking-away lesson is a teaching claim without source support.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The prayer/deliverance lesson goes beyond recall and needs source support.
[agent:needs_rewrite] Good image, but the hardened-heart lesson adds extra interpretation.
[agent:needs_source] The false-repentance teaching lacks a verifiable public source; use a pure recall hook.
[agent:needs_rewrite] The dust hook is strong, but the “lowliest element against the proud” line adds interpretation.
[agent:needs_source] Useful creation-from-dust teaching, but it needs Scripture source support.
[agent:keep] Memorable “three and done” hook that accurately marks the magicians’ failure.
[agent:needs_source] Good Bible-wide phrase hook, but the Sinai and Luke connections need source support.
[agent:keep] Short mnemonic with a clear fourth-plague fly hook.
[agent:keep] “Goshen = Go safe” is sticky and directly answers the region question.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The Shepherd/Passover typology needs source support and lengthens the aid.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The half-deliverance lesson is a teaching claim without source support.
[agent:keep] Clear order mnemonic, with the gnats as a memorable hinge.
